revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:網走神社]]


{{Unreferenced|date=September 11, 2017|qq=}}
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|name   = Abashiri Shrine
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|image  = File:Abashiri-jinja06n.jpg
|location = {{ill|Abashiri|en|Abashiri|qid=Q305640|lt=Abashiri, Hokkaido|1=Abashiri}}, Katsura Town 2-1-1, Hokkaido
|coordinates = {{Coord|44.015722|N|144.270194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|deity = {{ill|Three Goddesses of Munakata|qid=Q10948069|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}
|founded = {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era|1=Bunka}} 9 (1812)
|type = Former Prefectural Shrine
|festival = August 15
|Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri
|map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=44|lat_min=0|lat_sec=56.6|lon_deg=144|lon_min=16|lon_sec=12.7|label=Abashiri Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg|qq=}}
|location = {{ill|Abashiri|qid=Q305640|1=Abashiri}}
}}
[[File:Abashiri-jinja05s3.jpg|thumb|Prince Shōtoku Hall]]
'''Abashiri Shrine''' (網走神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Abashiri|en|Abashiri|qid=Q305640|lt=Abashiri, Hokkaido|1=Abashiri}}, Hokkaido. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=近代社格制度|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=近代社格制度|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}.
The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=イチキシマヒメ|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=イチキシマヒメ|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=タキリビメ|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=タキリビメ|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=タキリビメ}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/ja|13=User:Immanuelle/タギツヒメ|13=User:Immanuelle/zh|13=User:Immanuelle/多岐都比賣命|13=User:Immanuelle/fr|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/ja|13=User:Immanuelle/タギツヒメ|13=User:Immanuelle/zh|13=User:Immanuelle/多岐都比賣命|13=User:Immanuelle/fr|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle/ja|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle/タギツヒメ|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle/zh|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle/多岐都比賣命|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle/fr|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, commonly referred to as {{ill|Benzaiten|en|Benzaiten|qid=Q818468|qid=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=Benzaiten}}. {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} is also enshrined as a supplementary deity.
== History ==
The shrine traces its origins to {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era|1=Bunka}} 9 (1812), when a man from {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|qid=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}, named Shirobei Fujino, established a small shrine to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=弁才天|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=弁才天|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=弁才天}} near the mouth of the {{ill|Abashiri River|en|Abashiri River|qid=Q3650456|lt=Abashiri River|1=Abashiri River}} as a guardian deity of the fishing grounds. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, settlers in Abashiri formed a village and persuaded the Fujino family to transfer the shrine to them as a local guardian deity and {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|qid=Q767655|lt=Ujigami|1=Ujigami}}.
After several relocations, a new shrine building was constructed in 1908 at its current location. The shrine officially received a divided spirit from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|qid=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} and was recognized as a shrine without Rank (mukakusha). In 1923, it was elevated to {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=近代社格制度|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=近代社格制度|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, and after major construction work in 1929, it was promoted to {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=近代社格制度|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=近代社格制度|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} in 1934.
== Facilities ==
[[File:Abashiri-gokoku-jinja01s3.jpg|thumb|Abashiri Gokoku Shrine]]
The precinct includes the {{ill|Abashiri Gokoku Shrine|ja|網走護国神社|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abashiri Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abashiri Gokoku Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abashiri Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=網走神社|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=網走神社|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=網走神社|1=Abashiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata}}, which enshrines 705 individuals from the Abashiri sub-prefecture who perished in national service.
In 1981, seven {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|qid=Q846858|lt=Shichifukujin|1=Seven Lucky Gods}} statues previously dispersed across seven locations in the city were gathered and enshrined at Abashiri Gokoku Shrine. The statues were restored and installed within the shrine grounds.
== Surroundings ==
* {{ill|Katsuragaoka Park|ja|桂ヶ岡公園|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragaoka Park|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragaoka Park|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragaoka Park|1=Katsuragaoka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11536547}}
** {{ill|Katsuragaoka Chashi|en|Katsuragaoka Chashi|qid=Q11536550|lt=Katsuragaoka Chashi|1=Katsuragaoka Chashi}}
** {{ill|Abashiri City Folk Museum|en|Abashiri City Folk Museum|qid=Q11607220|lt=Abashiri City Folk Museum|1=Abashiri City Folk Museum}}
* {{ill|Hōryūji (Abashiri)|ja|法龍寺|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji (Abashiri)|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji (Abashiri)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryūji (Abashiri)|1=Hōryūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11555406}}
